home.title: Extract Keywords from Text or URL
home.subtitle: Free online keyword extraction tool — Instant results, no signup required
home.placeholderText: Paste or type your text here...
home.noResults: Enter text or URL and click extract to see results
home.seoHowTitle: How the Website Keyword Extractor Works
home.seoHowDesc1: Our keyword extraction tool analyzes your text to identify the most important terms and phrases. Using word frequency analysis, stop-word filtering, and multi-word phrase detection, it surfaces the keywords that best represent your content.
home.seoHowDesc2: Simply paste your text or enter a URL, click "Extract Keywords," and receive a ranked list of keywords with their frequency count and density percentage.
home.seoHowStep1Title: Paste Your Text or Enter a URL
home.seoHowStep1Desc: Open the Text tab to paste your content directly, or switch to the URL tab to enter any publicly accessible webpage address. The URL extractor automatically fetches the page content so you don't have to copy and paste manually.
home.seoHowStep2Desc: Our tool performs word frequency analysis, filters out common stop words, and identifies multi-word phrases including bigrams and trigrams so you get a complete picture of your content's keyword profile.
home.seoHowStep3Desc: Browse results sorted by frequency and density. Use filter tabs to view one-word, two-word, or three-word phrases. Download as CSV or copy to clipboard for use in your SEO tools and spreadsheets.
home.seoWhyDesc: Keyword extraction is essential for SEO optimization, content analysis, and research. Understanding which terms dominate your content helps you align with search intent, improve readability, and target the right audience. Marketers, writers, and researchers use keyword extraction to validate content strategy and discover untapped opportunities.
home.seoWhy1: SEO Optimization — Identify focus keywords for your content
home.seoWhy2: Competitor Analysis — Understand what keywords competitors target
home.seoWhy3: Content Strategy — Plan your content around high-value keywords
home.seoWhy4: Market Research — Discover trending topics in your niche
home.seoFaq1A: Yes, the Text and URL extraction modes are completely free. No signup or account required. AI-powered semantic extraction is planned as a Pro feature for users who need deeper contextual analysis.
home.seoFaq2Q: How accurate is the keyword extraction?
home.seoFaq2A: The tool uses word frequency, stop-word filtering, keyword density, and multi-word phrase detection (bigrams and trigrams) to identify meaningful keywords. This approach works well for most English text where keyword frequency and phrase patterns are useful. AI-powered semantic extraction, which uses contextual understanding for more nuanced results, is planned as a future Pro feature.
home.seoFaq3Q: Can I extract keywords from any website?
home.seoFaq3A: The URL mode can extract content from most publicly accessible web pages. Some sites with paywalls, login requirements, or anti-scraping measures may not be accessible. For best results, copy the text directly and use the Text tab.
home.seoFaq4Q: What formats can I download the results in?
home.seoFaq4A: Results can be downloaded as a CSV file (compatible with Excel and Google Sheets) or copied to your clipboard as tab-separated values for quick pasting into any application.
home.seoHowToUseTitle: How to Extract Keywords from Text or URL in 3 Simple Steps
home.seoUseSeoDesc: Analyze competitor content and identify target keywords on any webpage using our SEO keyword extractor. Verify your own content strategy by running pages through the URL extractor to see which terms appear most often, and uncover content gaps your competitors overlook.
home.seoUseContentDesc: Paste your draft article to extract keywords from text and verify your target terms appear naturally. Find keywords in text you've already written to identify missing topics, and optimize posts for search engines before publishing.
home.seoUseAcademicDesc: Apply keyword extraction for NLP research tasks and document analysis. Extract keywords from documents, research papers, or literature to quickly identify main themes, track topic shifts across collections, and build keyword indices for systematic reviews.
home.seoUseMarketingDesc: Audit landing pages and competitor websites by extracting keywords from web pages without manual review. Use the website keyword extractor to analyze any URL and understand the keyword profile of both your own pages and competitive targets.
home.seoHowStatsDesc: The extractor counts how many times each word appears in your text and calculates density as a percentage of total word count. Higher-frequency words rank higher, giving you a clear picture of the dominant terms and themes in any body of text. According to SEO industry research, multi-word phrases account for roughly 70% of all search queries, making phrase-level extraction essential for meaningful keyword analysis.
home.seoHowFilterDesc: Common English words like 'the,' 'is,' and 'at' carry little semantic meaning and are automatically removed using a built-in stop-word list. This ensures your results contain only meaningful terms, not filler words that appear frequently but add no SEO value.
home.seoHowPhraseDesc: Beyond single keywords, the tool detects and ranks two-word phrases (bigrams) and three-word phrases (trigrams) that appear together. These multi-word combinations often represent more specific and valuable search terms, capturing the full context of how words are used.
home.seoHowUrlNote: For URL extractions, our tool fetches the publicly accessible HTML of the webpage, respects robots.txt directives, and extracts the main text content before running the same keyword analysis used in Text mode. Just paste a URL and get results instantly — no signup needed.
home.seoFaq5Q: What’s the difference between regular and AI keyword extraction?
home.seoFaq5A: Regular keyword extraction, available now for free, uses word frequency analysis, keyword density, stop-word filtering, and phrase detection (bigrams and trigrams) to identify the most common terms in your text. AI semantic extraction is planned as a Pro feature for future release. Until then, the free Text and URL modes provide keyword extraction without signup.
home.seoFaq6Q: Do I need to sign up to use the keyword extractor?
home.seoFaq6A: No signup is required for the free Text and URL extraction modes. You can paste text or enter any publicly accessible URL and get keyword results instantly. AI-powered semantic extraction is planned for a future Pro plan, which may require an account. All free features will remain available without registration.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.highlightText: Free text and URL extraction requests are processed in real time and immediately discarded. We do not store pasted content, fetched page text, submitted URLs, or extracted results.

metadata.title: Privacy Policy
metadata.description: ExtractKeywords privacy policy: how we handle data for free keyword extraction. No account required, no cookies, no tracking.
metadata.openGraphDescription: How ExtractKeywords handles your data during free keyword extraction. No account, no cookies, no storage of submitted content.
metadata.schemaDescription: ExtractKeywords privacy policy: no account required, no cookies, no tracking. Submitted text and URL content are processed in real time and never stored.

overview.title: Overview
paragraphs: ExtractKeywords is a free keyword extraction tool available at extractkeywords.com. No account or signup is required. You can paste text or enter a publicly accessible URL, and our tool generates keyword frequency, density, and phrase analysis results in real time.
This Privacy Policy describes what information we process, how we handle it, and your rights. By using ExtractKeywords, you agree to the practices described below.

information.title: Information We Process
paragraphs: ExtractKeywords is designed to process data without storing it. Here is what we process and when:

usage.title: How We Use Information
paragraphs: We process information only for these purposes:

list.label: Provide extraction results —
list.text: Process pasted text or fetched URL content to generate keyword frequency, density, and phrase detection results for the current request. Results are displayed in your browser and not retained.

list.label: Maintain and protect the service —
list.text: Rate limiting, abuse monitoring, and anonymous server log analysis help us keep the tool available and secure for everyone.

list.label: Respond to support requests —
list.text: If you contact us, we use your message content solely to address your inquiry.

cookies.title: Cookies & Local Storage
paragraphs: ExtractKeywords does not use cookies. The only data stored on your device is your theme preference (light, dark, or system), which is saved to your browser's local storage. This is a functional necessity for a consistent user experience and contains no personal information. You can clear it at any time through your browser settings.

We do not use tracking cookies, analytics cookies, advertising cookies, or any third-party tracking technologies on this site. No data is shared with advertising networks, analytics providers, or social media platforms.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
paragraphs: ExtractKeywords relies on the following third-party services to operate:

security.title: Data Security
paragraphs: We implement the following measures to protect your data and maintain service availability:

list.text: All communication with our servers is encrypted over HTTPS.
Free extraction input and results are processed in real time and are not stored after the request completes.
URL extraction is sandboxed: only http/https schemes are accepted, private and internal IP ranges are blocked (SSRF protection), and fetched content is size-limited.
Rate limiting is applied to prevent abuse and ensure fair access for all users.
The free tier is capped at 10,000 characters per submission. Larger submissions are rejected before processing.

rights.title: Your Rights
paragraphs: Under applicable data protection laws, you may have the right to:

list.label: Be informed —
list.text: This Privacy Policy explains what information we process and how.

list.label: Access your data —
list.text: Request confirmation of whether we hold any personal data relating to you.

list.label: Request erasure —
list.text: Request deletion of any personal data we may hold about you (e.g., server logs).

list.label: Lodge a complaint —
list.text: File a complaint with your local data protection authority if you believe your rights have been violated.

contact.title: Contact Us
paragraphs: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise your data rights, please reach out:

list.label: Email:
list.text: support@extractkeywords.com
We make every effort to respond within 30 days where required by applicable law.

changes.title: Policy Changes
paragraphs: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or for operational, legal, or regulatory reasons. When we make changes, we will update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page.
Continued use of ExtractKeywords after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

cells: Processed in real time by the keyword extraction engine (word frequency, density, phrase detection). The raw text is not stored after results are returned to you.

The URL is fetched over HTTPS. Only publicly accessible HTML is retrieved. Response size is capped. Private/internal IP addresses are rejected. The fetched text is extracted and processed, then discarded.
Standard server logs (timestamp, request path, response status, approximate IP address) collected by Vercel as part of its hosting infrastructure.
Cloud hosting and content delivery. ExtractKeywords is deployed on Vercel's global edge network.
Vercel processes standard HTTP request data (IP address, request path, timestamp, response status) as part of its hosting infrastructure. Refer to Vercel's privacy policy for details.
Typeface rendering in the browser. Font files are self-hosted and served from the same domain as the application.
No external requests are made at runtime. Font files were downloaded at build time and are served directly by Vercel.

metadata.title: Terms of Service
metadata.description: ExtractKeywords terms of service: rules and conditions for using our free keyword extraction tool.
metadata.openGraphDescription: Terms governing the use of ExtractKeywords free keyword extraction tool.
metadata.schemaDescription: ExtractKeywords terms of service for free keyword extraction.

introduction.title: Introduction
paragraphs: ExtractKeywords provides a free online keyword extraction service accessible at extractkeywords.com. These Terms of Service govern your use of the Service.
Please read these terms carefully before using the Service.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
paragraphs: By accessing or using ExtractKeywords, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use the Service.
We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of the Service after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

service.title: Service Description
paragraphs: ExtractKeywords offers keyword extraction by analyzing text or URL content using word frequency analysis, stop-word filtering, and multi-word phrase detection. The Service is free to use. No account or payment is required.
The free tier is limited to 10,000 characters per submission. URL extraction works only on publicly accessible web pages and respects robots.txt directives. Only http and https URLs are accepted.
Extraction results are generated in real time and are not stored on our servers. The Service is intended for lawful purposes only.

eligibility.title: Eligibility
paragraphs: By using the Service, you represent and warrant that:

list.label: Age —
list.text: You are at least 13 years of age, or older if required by your country of residence.

list.label: Authority —
list.text: You have the legal capacity to enter into these terms.

list.label: Compliance —
list.text: You will use the Service in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

restrictions.title: Prohibited Uses
paragraphs: You agree not to:

list.label: Automated access —
list.text: Use bots, scrapers, or automated tools to access or extract data from the Service beyond normal usage.

list.label: Abusive content —
list.text: Submit content that is illegal, harmful, or infringes on the rights of others.

list.label: Circumvention —
list.text: Attempt to bypass rate limits, security measures, or technical restrictions of the Service.

list.label: Misrepresentation —
list.text: Use the Service in a way that misrepresents the origin or nature of extracted content.

list.label: Rate limit abuse —
list.text: Send excessive requests that disrupt the availability or performance of the Service for other users.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property
paragraphs: The ExtractKeywords name, logo, and tool interface are owned by ExtractKeywords. The Service itself is provided for you to use — you may not copy, modify, or redistribute the tool or its underlying technology.
Content you submit for extraction remains yours. We do not claim ownership over the text, URLs, or extracted results you generate using the Service.

proFeatures.title: Pro Features
paragraphs: AI-powered semantic extraction is planned as a paid Pro feature. When available, Pro will be offered at $9.99 per month or $99.9 per year. Payments will be processed by Creem Inc.
Details about Pro features, pricing, and availability will be announced when the feature launches.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
paragraphs: ExtractKeywords is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. To the fullest extent permitted by law:

list.text: We disclaim all warranties regarding the accuracy, reliability, or availability of the Service.
We are not liable for any damages arising from your use of the Service.
The Service may be interrupted for maintenance, updates, or unforeseen circumstances.
We are not responsible for the accuracy, legality, or availability of content fetched from third-party websites via the URL extraction feature.

list.label: Email:
list.text: support@extractkeywords.com

changes.title: Changes to Terms
paragraphs: We reserve the right to modify these Terms of Service at any time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated "Last updated" date.
Your continued use of ExtractKeywords after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the modified terms.

about.title: About ExtractKeywords

metadata.title: About
metadata.description: Learn about ExtractKeywords — a free keyword extraction tool built for speed, privacy, and real-world SEO and content analysis.
metadata.openGraphDescription: Learn about ExtractKeywords, the free online keyword extraction tool. No signup, no cookies.
metadata.schemaDescription: ExtractKeywords is a free keyword extraction tool. No account required, no cookies, no tracking.

about.whatDesc: ExtractKeywords is a free online tool that analyzes text or webpage content to identify the most important keywords and phrases. Using word frequency analysis, stop-word filtering, keyword density calculations, and multi-word phrase detection (bigrams and trigrams), it surfaces the terms that best represent your content.
about.whatDesc2: The tool is designed for SEO professionals, content creators, researchers, and digital marketers who need fast, reliable keyword extraction without account creation or feature-limited trials. Paste your text or enter a publicly accessible URL and get results instantly.
about.whatDesc3: The free tier supports submissions of up to 10,000 characters. URL extraction is available for publicly accessible web pages and respects robots.txt directives. Only http and https URLs are accepted, with built-in SSRF protection.
about.principlesCardFreeDesc: The Text and URL extraction modes are completely free. No signup, no credit card, no account needed.
about.principlesCardPrivacyDesc: We do not use cookies or tracking scripts. Submitted content is processed in real time and discarded. Your data stays yours.
about.principlesCardPracticalDesc: Keyword frequency, density, and phrase detection are designed for SEO professionals, content creators, researchers, and marketers.
about.whyDesc: Keyword extraction is a well-understood technical problem: word frequency analysis, stop-word filtering, and n-gram detection are established algorithms. Yet most online tools wrap this functionality behind paywalls, mandatory account registration, or intrusive tracking.
about.whyDesc2: ExtractKeywords was built to provide a clean, focused alternative: a tool that does one thing well and leaves no trace. No signup flow, no data retention, no analytics scripts. Paste your text, get your keywords, and move on.

comparison.title: Compare Features
comparison.caption: Feature comparison between Free and Pro plans

faq.question: Is there a Pro plan with more features?
faq.answer: Yes, a Pro plan is planned with unlimited character limits, AI-powered keyword extraction, PDF and YouTube extraction, 30-day history, and priority support. Final availability will be announced before launch.

faq.question: How much will Pro cost?
faq.answer: Pro is planned at $9.99 per month or $99 per year (save $20). Exact pricing may change before launch. We'll announce final pricing closer to launch.

faq.question: What features will Pro include?
faq.answer: Pro is planned to include: unlimited character limit per submission, AI-powered semantic keyword extraction, PDF document keyword extraction, YouTube video keyword extraction, 30-day extraction history, and priority support. The final feature set will be confirmed closer to launch.

faq.question: What is the free plan limit?
faq.answer: The free plan supports up to 10,000 characters per submission with text and URL keyword extraction, bigram and trigram analysis, CSV download, and clipboard copy. No account or signup required.

faq.question: Do I need an account to use the tool?
faq.answer: No. The free Text and URL extraction modes work without any account or signup. An account may be needed for future Pro features.

cta.title: Not sure yet?
cta.subtitleRest: — no signup, no credit card. Pro is planned for when you need more.
